bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use SpriteSheetBundle for the player state atlas and swap animation clips on one UmbraPlayer entity.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/player_umbra_idle_6f_12fps.png during Loading and register the six-frame atlas under the Umbra idle state key.
runtime_notes: Keep the pivot centered on the torso so run, jump, grab, and exposure variants inherit consistent collider alignment.
The eye glow should remain visible without exceeding the silhouette-first art direction.
Idle motion must feel alive but restrained because puzzle planning happens while the player stands still.

context.usage: Default grounded state when the player is safe in shadow and not currently moving, jumping, or grabbing objects.

shape.silhouette: Small shadow creature with tapered head crest, bright eye pair, rounded torso, and a cloak-like lower body that frays into wisps.

specs.texture: Mostly solid silhouette with one soft teal inner rim near the eyes and upper torso.
specs.shading: Sharp shadow mass with minimal edge glow only where the form would separate from the background.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 192x48 px with six 32x48 frames.
The eye glow remains readable in every frame while the outer silhouette stays predominantly black.
All frames fit inside a stable 32x48 footprint so physics alignment does not shift between states.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds puzzle rules, object dimensions, HUD placement, and the Bevy 0.14 technical targets for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art-deco tower mood, silhouette layering, amber beam contrast, and architectural ornament rhythm.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra silhouette proportions, eye-glow placement, and cloak-like shadow contours for player and echo scale checks.
